caption: c
description: Radar plot comparing the quantitative performance metrics (Responsivity, Noise Equivalent Power, bandwidth, and various mechanical strengths) of two materials, Germanium (Ge) and Silicon (Si), showing their relative performance across seven distinct axes using filled polygons.
legend: Overall performance evaluation of the resulting optoelectronic fibres. The NEP value of the Si optoelectronic fibres is amplified by a factor of ten for visualization. For measurement of responsivity and NEP, n = 9 for all cases. For other measurements, n = 6 for all cases. All data are presented as mean ± s.d.
seo_title: Material Comparison Mechanical Strength Radar Plot, Nature 2025

caption: e
description: Three distinct panels displaying the distribution and mean comparison of Microglial volume (Iba1), CD68 content, and NET content between APP^NL-G-F mice and APP^NL-G-F x TSPO-KO mice. Statistical comparisons are shown via brackets and P-values (e.g., p=0.0437 for NET content).
legend: Analysis of NET volume, Iba1 volume and CD68 volume. App NL-G-F x TSPO-KO microglia contain less NET + signal than App NL-G-F microglia. ( n = 3 C57BL/6 J vs. n = 3 App NL-G-F , each 5 pictures).
seo_title: Microglia volume Microglial activation Box Plot, Nature Communications 2025

caption: d
description: A time series line plot illustrating tumour volume growth (in mm³) over 24 days across four treatment conditions (Control, FUS, Dox, and the combined Dox + FUS), demonstrating superior efficacy of the combination therapy.
legend: Tumour volume measurements over time using caliper-based assessments. Adjusted P values at end-point are as follows: in d , P = 0.0076; in h , P = 0.0037. Each line represents the group mean ± s.e.m. ( n = 4 mice per group). Statistical significance was determined using two-way ANOVA with Tukey’s multiple comparisons test (two-sided). ** and **** denote adjusted P < 0.01 and P < 0.0001, respectively.
seo_title: Tumour Volume Drug Efficacy Combination Therapy Line Plot, Nature Materials 2025

caption: c
description: A single-cell gene expression Dot Plot (Bubble Heatmap) visualizing the average expression (color intensity) and the percentage of expressing cells (dot size) for various marker genes across 28 distinct cell clusters, categorized into Cardiomyocyte (CM), Endothelial Cell (EC), and Fibroblast/Mesenchymal (MC-FB) lineages.
legend: Enrichment of canonical cell type markers across coarse-grained single-cell clusters. Dashed rectangles highlight consensus markers of CMs, ECs and FBs.
seo_title: Gene Expression Single-Cell RNA Sequencing Dot Plot, Nature Genetics 2025

caption: a
description: Detailed computational pipeline showing the training label curation (CTRP, CCLE), multi-stage feature engineering (ssGSEA from MSigDB), and predictor generation using a weighted ensemble of nine machine learning algorithms (LightGBM, KNN, Neural Nets). The workflow concludes with downstream applications for drug response prediction and tumor immune microenvironment interpretation.
legend: The process consists of four key components: (1) Data Preprocessing  standardization and labeling of the training dataset, (2) Feature Engineering  derivation of relevant features for robust glioma drug response prediction, (3) Training Module  a comprehensive process involving initial training, feature reduction, and performance assessment for individual predictor development, and (4) Downstream Applications  functionalities such as drug response predictions and microenvironment interpretations.
seo_title: Machine Learning Drug Response Prediction Flowchart, Cell Discovery 2025

caption: a
description: A schematic illustrating the GLUE PAIRED computational pipeline for single-cell data integration (scATAC-seq and scRNA-seq), guided by a knowledge-based graph, followed by MOFA INFERENCE steps including variational approximation (ELBO calculation) and factor matrix decomposition.
legend: The representation includes a graphical model of cell type correlations using GLUE, alongside an illustration of cell variance captured by MOFA, as indicated by the Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO).
seo_title: Multi-modal integration scATAC-seq scRNA-seq Schematic diagram, Nature Communications 2025

caption: b
description: A meta-analysis summary plot displaying the Log Response Ratio (LRR) shift as the central estimate, represented by colored dots. Horizontal segments depict the confidence intervals. The data is grouped hierarchically into Biome, Pressure, Organism, and Scale, with dot size likely encoding the sample size (N, shown in parentheses).
legend: log-response ratio of community composition shift (LRR shift). The global response (all data) is shown on the first row of each panel and is separated by factors in the following rows. The numbers in parentheses indicate the number of comparisons. For each category the dot represents the marginal mean computed from the model; dot size is proportional to number of studies included. The larger bar shows the 95% confidence interval and the thinner bar represents the 99% confidence interval.
seo_title: Log Response Ratio Biome Ecological Pressure Cleveland Dot Plot, Nature 2025

caption: a
description: A comprehensive study design flowchart outlining the process for two-sample Mendelian Randomization (MR) using UK Biobank data. The workflow analyzes Leukocyte Telomere Length (LTL) and various traits, including steps for data harmonization, observational correlation models, causal inference (MR LTL on trait, MR trait on LTL), sensitivity analyses (pleiotropy), and multivariable MR/mediation analysis. Includes embedded Manhattan and Line plots.
seo_title: Mendelian Randomization GWAS Summary Statistics Flowchart, Genome Biology 2025

caption: d
description: Six principal component analysis (PCA) plots, faceted by specific marker genes, showing individual cell observations mapped onto PC1 and PC2. The continuous color scale represents the normalized expression level of each gene, highlighting Glial and Neuronal differentiation trajectories originating from a Schwann cell progenitor state.
legend: Expression of glial and neuronal differentiation markers.
seo_title: Principal Component Analysis Gene Expression Mapping PCA Plot, Nature Genetics 2025

caption: a
description: A combination plot using violin plots, box plots, and overlaid scatter points to show the distribution of 'Change in habitat suitability' across six distinct future scenarios defined by Year (2055, 2095) and Scenario code (126, 370, 585). The overall distribution shows a significant difference (P < 0.001) across the groups.
legend: Projected change in habitat suitability from baseline (absolute, 2005–2019) for 14 LMEs defined as medium importance, in which the result from a Kruskal–Wallis rank-sum test is shown at top left ( ² = 32.00, P = 5.93 × 10 -6 ). Circles denote individual LME values, the thick line denotes the median and boxes bound the interquartile range (25th to 75th percentile), with whiskers extending to the maximum and minimum values. Upper and lower boundaries of violin plots extend to the maximum and minimum values, respectively, and width represents the density of observations.
seo_title: Habitat suitability Climate scenario modeling Violin Plot, Nature Climate Change 2025

caption: c
description: A time series line plot showing the persistence and radiance efficiency of IL-12 delivery systems (Ni LbL and Mal LbL) compared to free IL-12 over 100 hours. The Y-axis is log-scaled, and data includes error bars.
legend: B6C3F1 mice (n = 8 animals per group for 0–24 h and n = 3 animals per group for 24–96 h) inoculated with 10 6 HM-1-luc tumour cells on day 0 were administered fluorescently tagged NPs carrying 20 µg of IL-12 (or an equivalent dose of free IL-12) on day 14. Whole-animal imaging NP fluorescence (a) and IL-12 fluorescence for UL (b) and LbL (c) NP treatments are shown from the i.p. space collected over time post-dosing (mean ± s.d.).
seo_title: Radiance efficiency Time course Interleukin-12 Line Plot, Nature Materials 2025

caption: f
description: A multi-panel visualization using 100% stacked bar plots to illustrate the proportional abundance of various cell subtypes (Cardiomyocytes, Endothelial Cells, and Fibroblasts) across four distinct developmental periods (PCW). Triangular markers on the right summarize the magnitude and direction of proportional change between the earliest and latest time points.
legend: Temporal changes in coarse-grained cluster proportions in the CM, EC and MC–FB subsets across four developmental age groups (5.5–6, 7–8, 9–11 and 12–14 PCW). 2x, 3x, 4x increase; 2x, 3x, 4x decrease between the 5.5–6 and 12–14 PCW age groups.
seo_title: Cell proportion Cell subtype analysis Stacked Bar Plot, Nature Genetics 2025

caption: b
description: A multi-panel figure displaying spectral data. The top panels show extinction cross-section spectra (σext) against Energy (eV) for Copper and Silver, where multiple lines are vertically offset based on the continuous variable Plasmon Radius (R), indicated by a blue-to-red color gradient. The bottom panel is a scatter plot showing the relationship between renormalized energy (ħΩR) and plasma frequency (ωp) for four different metals.
legend: Rabi splitting of different nano-objects vs plasma frequency ω p . The line shows a linear fit from the analytical model.
seo_title: Extinction cross-section Energy spectrum Line Plot, Nature Communications 2025

caption: b
description: Comparative bimodal probability density plots showing the distribution of Chromatin-Lamina Affinity (V_LAD) under two mechanical conditions: SOFT and STIFF substrates. The distributions are partitioned into Small and Large LADs. Schematic arrows overlay the visualization, illustrating how Contractility inhibition (Y27), ECM degeneration, HDAC inhibition (TSA), or substrate stiffening regulate the distribution shifts between the soft and stiff states.
legend: The nucleus-wide distribution of the strength of chromatin-lamina affinity extracted from our framework shows a bimodal distribution with one peak at vanishing chromatin-lamina interactions with small discrete LADs (labelled small LADs) and another at strong chromatin-lamina interactions, comparable to the chromatin-chromatin interactions, with large continuous LADs (labelled large LADs). On a stiff substrate reduced nuclear HDAC3 contributes to decreasing the chromatin-lamina interaction strength. Note that the peak corresponding to large LADs decreases. Also summarizes the effects of pharmacological perturbations (TSA, Y27 treatments), change in substrate stiffness and in-vivo tendinopathic ECM degeneration on the distribution of the chromatin-lamina interaction strengths.
seo_title: Chromatin-lamina affinity Lamina Associated Domains Density Plot, Nature Communications 2025

caption: d
description: Faceted Density Plots showing the pooled effect sizes (Signature value, z-score) compared across three longitudinal time points: Baseline, 4 year, and 9 year. Each panel compares the time point distribution against a reference (grey) distribution, annotating sample size (n), Cohen's d (effect size), and P-values.
legend: Pooled effect sizes from c , measured by Cohen’s d , compare baseline diagnoses or diagnoses developing by 4 or 9 years with diagnosis-free individuals. A two-sided Wilcoxon rank-sum test used to compute P values.
seo_title: Effect sizes Pooled analysis Longitudinal data Density Plot, Nature Human Behaviour 2025

caption: b
description: Faceted comparative Box Plots displaying expression levels (Log2 CPM) and Allele-Specific Expression (ASE) ratios for X-linked NDD genes (ALG13, EIF2S3, KDM6A, PLP1, TFE3, USP9X) in hiPSCs and neurons, stratified by sex (Male) and X-Chromosome Inactivation (XCI) status (Female Groups 1, 2, 3).
legend: Boxplots of median ASE for the disease genes show higher ASE in group 3 female lines.

caption: d
description: Bar plot displaying Normalized Response to a 'Pinch' stimulus across various sensory neuron types (LTMRs) and specific receptors (MRGPR, SSTR2, TRPM8). Bars represent aggregated central tendency, overlaid with individual jittered data points and associated error bars.
legend: Summaries of response amplitudes (ΔF/F) for pinch stimuli, normalized to each neuron’s maximum responses to all stimuli. Neuron counts from left to right are 33, 91, 38, 98, 131, 71, 81, 81, 89, 58, and 128. Neurons of each subtype were imaged from 3 to 5 animals, age 4–6 weeks. Error bars, SD.

caption: b
description: A bar plot displaying the Mean Fluorescence Intensity (MFI) of PD1 expression on CD8 T cells across three distinct treatment groups (Dextrose, IL-12, and Mal LbL), highlighting a statistically significant difference (P=0.0098) between the Mal LbL group and a comparison group.
legend: Quantitation of PD1 MFI of T cells in tumour nodules
seo_title: PD1 MFI CD8 T cell Immunology Cytokine treatment Bar Plot, Nature Materials 2025

caption: b
description: A grouped bar plot showing the Total IL-12 radiance efficiency (photons s⁻¹)/(µW cm⁻²) g⁻¹ across two tissue types (UGT and Omentum) and five distinct experimental conditions (IL-12 control, Ni UL, Ni LbL, Mal UL, Mal LbL). The plot includes individual data points and error bars, with significance brackets indicated by P-values.
legend: weight-normalized tissue IL-12 fluorescence 1 day after dosing in UGT and omentum (mean ± s.d.).
seo_title: IL-12 Radiance Efficiency Tissue Distribution Bar Plot, Nature Materials 2025

caption: b
description: A multi-panel plot showing cavity conductivity (sigma 1) as a function of frequency (nu) across five decreasing electron density (n) values. The total spectral response (solid line) is decomposed using component fitting (dashed lines and filled areas), demonstrating spectral shift and Rabi splitting (2g).
legend: Experimentally obtained real part of the near-field terahertz conductivity spectra of a selection of measured graphene carrier densities (Supplementary Fig. 5). The data were fit to a sum of four Lorentzians as indicated by the shaded peaks.
seo_title: Cavity conductivity Electron density dependence Spectral Plot, Nature Physics 2025

caption: c
description: A simulation output displaying the trajectory of 'LB length' (a.u.) over 'LB time' (a.u.) for four different parameter settings (k0, k1, k2, k3). Each line includes a shaded area representing the associated variability or error band, demonstrating time-dependent kinetics.
legend: Time evolution of the distance between the k th nearest neighbours for the simulation data. The brighter areas indicate the standard deviation from the mean for the corresponding colour.
seo_title: Time Dependence Kinetics Parameter variation Time Series Plot, Nature Physics 2025

caption: g
description: A gene regulatory network graph showing core transcription factors (RORA, SETBP1, etc.) connected to their potential targets. Node coloring indicates gene function (Transcription Factor, Ion Channel) and differential expression status within the SAN-CM cluster compared to other Cardiomyocyte clusters.
legend: Gene regulatory network of SAN_CMs, including the top ten differentially expressed transcription factors compared to all other CM states and their associated target genes. la, left atrium; ra, right atrium; rv, right ventricle; ao, aorta; I f , funny current; I CaT , T-type calcium current; I CaL , L-type calcium current; I K , delayed rectifier potassium current; IK ACh , acetylcholine-activated potassium current.
seo_title: Gene Regulatory Network Transcription Factor Network Graph, Nature Genetics 2025

caption: b
description: A Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection (UMAP) plot showing the clustering of 73,946 single cells, labeled into 12 major cell types (e.g., Cardiomyocytes, Endothelial cells, and Mesenchymal cells/fibroblasts) with distinct sub-clusters demarcated by dashed lines and numerical labels.
legend: UMAP representing 31 coarse-grained cardiac single-cell clusters, corresponding to 11 main cell types, including CMs, ECs and non-mural MC–FBs (dashed lines).
seo_title: Single Cell Analysis Cell Type Identification UMAP, Nature Genetics 2025

caption: h
description: Multi-panel bar plots comparing the percentage of time spent freezing (Y-axis) across three behavioral stages (Habituation, Recall 1, Recall 2) under two primary induction states: DOX OFF (AcrIIA4 OFF) and DOX ON (AcrIIA4 ON). Individual data points and statistical significance markers are displayed.
legend: Compared to animals expressing dCas9-VPR plus Arc sgRNA and kept off DOX (n = 7), animals put ON DOX after the first recall to induce AcrIIA4 (n = 8) showed impaired memory retention, expressed as freezing percentage. Data are means  s.e.m. compared either by two-way ANOVA with Sidak’s post hoc test.
seo_title: Freezing Behavior Memory Recall Habituation Phase Bar Plot, Nature Genetics 2025

caption: c
description: A grouped bar plot comparing Lactate concentration (in µM) across two experimental conditions (LCO_5 and LCO_6), differentiated by three distinct groups: Control, S. nepalensis, and S. capitis. The plot utilizes error bars and statistical significance markers (asterisks) for comparison.
legend: Bar graphs showing lactate level of co-CM (9 h). Bars indicate the mean, and error bars denote the SD of three technical replicates. p values were calculated by two-sided Student’s t test;****p< 0.0001, ***p< 0.001, **p< 0.01.
seo_title: Lactate concentration Metabolite measurement Bar Plot, Cell Host & Microbe 2025

caption: a
description: Time series plot illustrating the historical and projected evolution of ocean temperature (200-700m depth on the continental shelf) from 1920 to 2090. Data is segmented by historical measurements and projected future scenarios based on Paris Agreement targets (1.5°C, 2°C) and Representative Concentration Pathways (RCP 4.5 and RCP 8.5). Uncertainty is displayed using continuous confidence ribbons.
legend: Temperature is averaged over the continental shelf and the depth range 200–700 m, and a 2-yr running mean is applied. For each scenario, the solid line denotes the ensemble mean, while the shaded region shows the full ensemble range. Dashed vertical lines show the onset of future scenarios (2006) and the time at which RCP 8.5 diverges from the other three scenarios (2045).
seo_title: Ocean Temperature Climate Change Modeling Time Series, Nature Climate Change 2025

caption: d
description: Four distinct expression patterns (Groups 2, 3, 4, 5) for gene BB2 are visualized. Each panel shows the average temporal expression profile using a Line Plot and the corresponding normalized FPKM values for individual accessions across five time points (4 DPA to 20 DPA) using a Heatmap.
legend: Expression profiles of Ghir_D05G007220 (BB2) in 340 accessions were divided into eight expression groups. The figure shows the four larger expression groups. Heatmaps showing normalized FPKM in each accession at each timepoint. Line charts showing the mean expression of accessions at each timepoint. The arrow points to the timepoint with the highest mean expression.
seo_title: Gene Expression Pattern Transcriptomics Heatmap, Nature Genetics 2025

caption: b
description: A multi-track Circos plot visualizing high-density genomic data across 12 distinct chromosomes (Chr1-Chr12). The visualization includes an outer chromosome ideogram (a) and three internal tracks (b, c, d) showing different genomic feature densities using radial line and bar representations.
legend: Distribution of A. suturalis genomic features. Track “a” represents the assembled chromosomes. Tracks “b–d” represents the distribution of GC density, repeat density, and gene density, respectively, with densities calculated in 500-kb windows
seo_title: Genomics Visualization Chromosome Mapping Circos Plot, BMC Biology 2025

caption: a
description: A square correlation heatmap displaying pairwise statistical relationships (Spearman or Pearson correlation coefficient) between the estimated abundance levels of various immune cell subtypes. The red-to-blue diverging colormap indicates positive (red) and negative (blue) correlation strengths, respectively, while asterisks mark statistical significance.
legend: Correlation heat map of immune infiltrating cell. The size of the colored squares represents the strength of the correlation; blue represents a negative correlation, and red represents a positive correlation. The darker the color is, the stronger correlation is.
seo_title: Immune Cell Composition Cell Abundance Correlation Heatmap, BMC Bioinformatics 2025

caption: a
description: A comprehensive flowchart outlining a multi-branch deep learning model architecture for object recognition. It shows a feature extractor (ResNeXt-FPN) feeding into three parallel modules: Spatial Context-aware Branch, Object-focus Branch, and Object-attentional Branch, which incorporate adaptive scaling, ROI pooling, and spatial-semantic attention before undergoing score-level fusion to produce predicted labels.
legend: Overview of the proposed SCO-DCNN. It consists of three branches: Object-focus Branch, Spatial Context-aware Branch and Object-attentional Branch. ResNeXt-101 with feature pyramid network is used as the backbone to generate the feature maps. Then, in the Object-focus Branch, a set of bounding boxes are generated for regions containing objects. In the Spatial Context-aware Branch, an adaptive patch-expansion mechanism is designed to expand tightly cropped patches to include the background contextual information, while avoiding excessive inclusion of the unnecessary background to increase intra-class variations. In the Object-attentional Branch, the spatially close and semantically related objects are grouped to form object clusters, and the spatial semantic information within clusters is encoded using the proposed spatial semantic attentional network.
seo_title: Deep Learning Model Object Recognition Flowchart, IEEE Transactions on Image Processing 2025

caption: e
description: Four volcano plots illustrating differential gene expression (DESeq log2 Beta vs. -log10 Adjusted p.value) across four distinct tissue comparisons: All tissues except Blood, Blood, Kidney/Lung/Liver/Pancreas, and IEL/LPL versus Spleen/LN/PP. Genes meeting thresholds (Log2 FC >= 2 or <= -2, and adjusted p.value <= 0.01) are highlighted in orange and individually labeled.
legend: Volcano plots of the differential expression analyses. Blood Treg cells are contrasted with the means of all other Treg cell samples, followed by pairwise contrasts of the remaining three tissue groups.
seo_title: Differential Gene Expression Transcriptomics Volcano Plot, Immunity 2025

caption: f
description: Multi-panel density plots displaying the distribution of the excitation-inhibition ratio across two major datasets (FAFB-FlyWire and hemibrain) and two neuronal locations (axon and dendrite), comparing three distinct, color-coded groups.
legend: Scaled density plots showing the distribution of excitation-inhibition balance (proportion of excitatory, acetylcholine, input minus the proportion of inhibitory input; GABA, glutamate) across neuron-level transmitter predictions and compartments. Vertical dashed line, median value. Colored boxes with stars indicate statistical comparisons, Wilcoxon two-sample tests (n.s., not significant;*p 0.05;***p 0.0001;****p 0.00001). See also Figures S2 and S3 and Data S3 and S4.
seo_title: Excitation-Inhibition Ratio Neuronal Compartment Density Plot, Cell 2025

caption: a
description: A horizontal bar chart displaying the ranking of 18 clinical, demographic, and socioeconomic variables based on their relative 'Importance' score (ranging from 0 to 100). The ranking is headed by Platelet count, Haematocrit, and liver transaminases.
legend: Random Forests enable examination of feature importances, which is the mean of the amount the Gini Index (or node impurity) decreases by in each tree at the split that uses the feature. The more the Gini Index decreases for a feature, the more important it is. This figure rates the features from 0-100, with 100 being the most important. EDD=expected date of delivery. GDP=gross domestic product. SpO2=oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry.
seo_title: Variable Importance Clinical Markers Horizontal Bar Plot, The Lancet Digital Health 2025

caption: d
description: Ridge plot displaying the distribution of the logarithmic GFP/DsRed fluorescence ratio, measured in percentage of cells, across eight different conditions. The conditions compare two reporters (mitoGFP and mitoGFPA6) under four different siRNA knockdowns (Ctrl, si_ClpP, si_Pirh2, and double knockdown).
legend: Flow cytometry analysis of RPE1 cells stably expressing mitoGFP or mitoGFPA6 treated with the indicated siRNAs.
seo_title: Flow Cytometry Analysis siRNA Knockdown Density Plot, Cell Reports 2025

caption: b
description: Distribution of 'Explained Variance' (EV) for neural prediction models, plotted horizontally for various Non-Human Primate (NHP) subjects. Each panel compares two conditions: Active versus Passive movement, using a combination of box plots and overlaid strip plots.
legend: Example distributions of single-neuron neural explainability for the best model layer (using the same model as (A) across all neurons for all NHPs and for active and passive movements. Each neuron is represented by a black dot and the neurons shown in (A) are highlighted.
seo_title: Neural Prediction Explained Variance Box Plot, Cell 2025

caption: e
description: UMAP visualization displaying the reduced dimensionality of high-dimensional single-cell data, showing distinct clusters of observations (putative cells) labeled numerically (18 through 41).
legend: Re-embedded 2D UMAP of 296,020 cells (glutamatergic neurons, GABAergic neurons, and spinal cord dorsal and ventral progenitors) from stages before E13.
seo_title: Single-cell data analysis Cell populations UMAP projection, Nature 2025

caption: l
description: Two time series plots showing the response dynamics of pC1x neuronal activity (measured by Delta F/F0) and the corresponding Tracking Index (T.I.) behavior over time, aligned with the delivery of an auditory 'song' stimulus.
legend: Same as (I) and (J), but showing the average activity of pC1x neurons.

caption: h
description: A visualization showing the overlap (intersections) of gene sets related to convergent evolution and positive selection under Deep-sea and Hadal conditions, supported by a schematic model defining the environmental zones and a bar plot summarizing total gene counts.
legend: Venn plot of the number of genes under convergent and positive selection under the deep-sea model and hadal model in 50 species.
seo_title: Convergent Evolution Positive Selection Venn Diagram, Cell 2025

caption: d
description: A stacked horizontal bar plot displaying the composition of different gene categories (Unclustered, various types of orthologs and paralogs) by count across several different samples or species. The total length of the bar represents the total number of genes analyzed in each sample.
legend: Distribution of different types of genes in the species shown in (C).
seo_title: Comparative Genomics Orthologs Paralogs Stacked Bar Plot, Current Biology 2025

caption: a
description: A spatial scatter plot displaying the distribution of two distinct populations of points (green and brown) relative to a continuous, smoothed boundary line (blue) and a flat reference line (black). The visualization is highly simplified, focusing on positional data, with a 1 cm spatial scale bar provided.
legend: Trail following by carpenter ants producing a sinuous path along a straight pheromone trail. Grey line: artificial odour trail. Thick blue line: path of ant head. Dots: positions of antennae. Adapted from54with permission from theJournal of Experimental Biology.
seo_title: Spatial distribution Boundary mapping Scatter points, Current Biology 2025

caption: d
description: A dot plot showing the distribution of delay times (ms) from a cue jump event to a subsequent reaction (SLT), categorized into three temporal segments: Prior, Peak/Trough, and Post. This is presented alongside behavioral schematics.
legend: The zigzag path of an ant trained to face a black-white edge. The ant makes a rapid turn (saccade-like turn) to fixate the edge when the edge is made to jump (bottom right). Top: the phase of the path is colour coded. The turns (saccade-like turns) only occur at the peaks of the ant’s path towards a feeder (+). Each saccade-like turn is shown as a dot with its proximity to a peak colour coded (bottom left). Even when delayed by several cycles, saccade-like turns still occur close to the peaks (adapted from58).
seo_title: Delay Time Measurement Temporal Segmentation Dot Plot, Current Biology 2025

caption: a
description: A complex schematic illustrating differential accumulation (Red: Accumulated, Blue: Decreased, Black: No Change) of nuclear-encoded mitochondrial proteins and enzymes across the inner and outer membranes (IM, OM) and matrix, comparing mature pollen to floral buds. Key processes shown include the TCA cycle, Electron Transport Chain (ETC), RNA editing complex activity, and protein import machinery.
seo_title: Mitochondrial function TCA cycle Schematic Diagram, Current Biology 2025

caption: a
description: Schematic illustration detailing a multi-step cancer immunotherapy mechanism involving SynNotch T cells. The process includes (1) Gene delivery to the tumor, (2) Activation of T cells via Focused Ultrasound (FUS) and Dox release in a 'Training centre', and (3) Subsequent massive killing of tumor cells. An inset panel magnifies the molecular events involving ATP release, Purinergic receptor signaling, and Ca2+ influx.
legend: Schematic diagram of the combinatorial immunotherapy strategy. First, tumours are transduced with the CaDox genetic system. Upon localized FUS stimulation, the CaDox system is activated in a subset of tumour cells, leading to ATP release via PANX1 hemichannels and calcium influx through purinergic receptors. This results in the expression of clinically validated antigens, such as tCD19, which prime synNotch CAR T cells (synNotch T). Activated synNotch CAR T cells then express anti-PSMA CAR to target and eliminate surrounding tumour cells.
seo_title: SynNotch T cell therapy Focused Ultrasound Schematic Diagram, Nature Materials 2025

caption: d
description: A 2D density plot visualizing the normalized conductivity ($\sigma_1$) as a function of carrier density and frequency, highlighting the dispersion relation and demonstrating mode splitting (Rabi splitting, labeled as $2g$) in a continuous parameter space.
legend: Simulation of a device as in b with a graphene monolayer and  = 1. An avoided crossing with  = 0.18 is observed.
seo_title: Carrier density function Frequency spectrum 2D Intensity Map, Nature Physics 2025

caption: g
description: A Bar Plot comparing the percentage of time spent freezing, segmented by sgRNA condition (NT vs Arc) and behavioral phase (Habituation vs Recall). Individual data points are shown overlaid on the mean bars, which include error bars and statistical significance annotations.
legend: Compared to animals injected with dCas9-KRAB-MeCP2 plus NT sgRNA ( n = 16), animals injected with dCas9-KRAB-MeCP2 plus Arc sgRNA ( n = 12) showed impaired memory recall. Data are means ± s.e.m. compared either by two-way ANOVA with Sidak’s and Fisher’s post hoc tests.
seo_title: Freezing behavior Habituation phase Recall phase Bar Plot, Nature Genetics 2025

Bar lengths represent the statistical significance ($-\log_{10}(P_{\text{adj}})$) of the associated biological process.
legend: Top ten unique biological process terms identified by GO enrichment analysis on the substantially differentially expressed genes of each KLF4 cluster (shown in c (right)).

description: A 2D spatial contour map visualizing Moiré periodicity (pM) across a region using a continuous red-to-blue color gradient scale (79.1 nm to 8.9 nm). The plot features a schematic overlay illustrating a cyclic process or measurement path (1 to 4).

caption: k
description: Four aggregated scatter plots displaying individual data points and summary statistics (Box Plots) to compare NET volume, Iba1-microglia volume, CD68 percentage, and NET percentage between control (C57BL/6J) and APP^NL-G-F (Alzheimer's model) mouse strains. P-values indicate significant differences for three of the four measured parameters.
legend: Analysis of NET volume, Iba1 volume and CD68 volume. App NL-G-F microglia contain more NET + signal than C57BL/6 J microglia ( n =3 C57BL/6 J vs. n =3 App NL-G-F , each 5 technical replicates); Data expressed as mean s.e.m.; ns, not significant; * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001, **** p < 0.0001; Unpaired, two-tailed t-test in ( b , k ); One-way ANOVA with Tukey’s post-hoc test in ( k ); Fig. 3d and e do not contain probability tests. Figure 3c depicts the results of a differential gene expression analysis from a quasi-likelihood negative binomial generalised log-linear model fitted to count data, and was corrected for multiple comparisons using the Benjamini-Hochberg FDR. Statistics shown in Supplementary Data 1 . Source data are provided as a Source Data file.
seo_title: Microglia Activation Iba1 Staining CD68 Expression Box Plot, Nature Communications 2025

caption: d
description: A scatter plot correlating Growth rate (Y-axis) against Activation rate (r, X-axis) for 51 individual patients. The data is dichotomized into two groups (grey and red), with the red group showing statistically significant deviation (p < 0.01) from a non-linear fitted curve, suggesting impairment in growth relative to activation.
legend: Model simulated activation- and inferred growth rates for n = 51 primary GBMs. The dotted line shows a logarithmic fit of growth rate among GBMs ( d ).
seo_title: Patient data analysis Growth rate measurement Scatter plot, Nature Communications 2025

caption: f
description: A faceted ridge plot illustrating the relationship between light Intensity and Wavelength (nm) across various measurement angles ($	heta_2$). The data, likely from spectroscopy, shows a sequential shift in the spectral shape and highlights a peak associated with Photonic Band Gap (PBG) phenomena.
legend: Angle-dependent FL and RTP spectra of R-PC gel.
seo_title: Spectroscopy Wavelength Intensity Ridge Plot, Nature Communications 2025

caption: f
description: A vertically stacked facet grid of two histograms comparing the likelihood distribution of 'Torque on turbine' under two distinct high/low concentration conditions (50 mM vs 3 M).
legend: Torque on the turbine measured by restraining its spin angle.
seo_title: Torque measurement Concentration dependence Histogram, Nature Nanotechnology 2025

caption: m
description: A Volcano plot showing the statistical significance (negative log P-value from log-rank test) versus the magnitude of effect (log Hazard Ratio, HR, from Cox regression) for various factors associated with Metastasis-free survival (MFS). Data points are colored and shaped to denote specific statistical methods and significance levels.
legend: Volcano plot for MFS based on urine proteins at time of clinical restaging ( n = 59, patients without samples or outcome data were excluded) showing log-rank test ( y axis) and Cox regression hazard ratio ( x axis). For associations with cCR, adjustment for multiple comparisons was performed using the BenjaminiHochberg method. FDR, false discovery rate; HR, hazard ratio; Nivo, nivolumab; NA, not applicable.
seo_title: Metastasis-free Survival Cox Hazard Ratio Scatter Plot, Nature Medicine 2025

caption: b
description: Two multi-panel horizontal bar plots (SHAP contribution plots) showing how behavioral application features contribute (positive or negative SHAP values) to a machine learning model's prediction score for individual participants (a neurotypical boy and an autistic girl). The bars are colored blue for neurotypical behavior contribution and orange for autistic behavior contribution.
legend: Examples of app administration reports are shown, one for a 25-month-old neurotypical boy and one for a 30-month-old autistic girl, both correctly classified, including each child’s app quality score, confidence score and the contributions of each app variable to the child’s individualized prediction.
seo_title: Explainable AI Machine Learning Prediction Horizontal Bar Plot, Nature Medicine 2025

caption: e
description: Faceted time series plots illustrating the dynamics of microbial Shannon diversity index over time (days post inoculation, dpi). Each panel displays scatter data and a regression fit with confidence intervals, comparing two community states (Growing and Stable). Spearman correlation coefficients and p-values quantify the relationship between time and diversity for each state within each condition.
legend: Spearman correlation between the Shannon diversity index and sampling time in growing (green) or stable (yellow) community states. Correlation coefficient ( R ) and p -value ( p ) are indicated with the colour according to the growing state. Curves represent the general additive model (GAM) fit (average, line) and the 95% confidence interval (shadow)
seo_title: Shannon Diversity Microbiome Dynamics Rhizosphere Time Series, Microbiome 2025

caption: g
description: Grouped bar chart quantifying the relative protein levels of Muc2, E-cadherin, and ZO-1 comparing two experimental conditions, CF and CR, with supporting Western blot images. The Y-axis shows a break for scale compression.
legend: Western blot of MUC2, E-cadherin, and ZO-1 in the colon. CF copper-free, CR copper-rich. Values are shown as means and error bars represent SEM. Statistical significance was determined using a two-tailed t test. * p <= 0.05, ** p <= 0.01, *** p <= 0.001
seo_title: Protein expression level Western blotting analysis Grouped Bar Plot, Microbiome 2025

caption: f
description: Faceted plot displaying the Electronic Density of States (DOS) as a function of Energy (eV) across five distinct quantum states (2Q to -2Q), illustrating a theoretical transition between the B2M and B2P phases in a material.
legend: The density of states plots of the AFM- b ground state (middle) and the modulated AFM- b state by B 2 M (top) and B 2 P (bottom).
seo_title: Electronic Density of States DOS Energy Ridge Plot, Nature Communications 2025

caption: b
description: A 5x5 confusion matrix visualized as a heatmap, comparing 'True labels' against 'Predicted labels' for five biological/cellular conditions (SNCA x3, Oligomer, Complex 1, Mitophagy, Control). The color intensity and numerical cell annotation represent classification accuracy, showing strong performance predominantly along the diagonal.
legend: Deep learning classification performance on an unseen test set trained on 8 x 8 tiled images by the confusion matrix.
seo_title: Classification Accuracy True Label Predicted Label Heatmap, Nature Machine Intelligence 2025

caption: c
description: A multi-panel box plot showing the distribution of 'Exposure' for seven different 'Copy number signatures'. Within each signature, samples are grouped and colored by their 'Relapse status after first line chemotherapy' (resistant, sensitive, no relapse), with individual data points overlaid. P-values comparing the groups are annotated in the facet labels.
legend: Variation in exposure to HGSOC copy-number signatures. Boxplot showing the exposure levels of the seven copy-number signatures of HGSOC for 215 samples from 46 patients, grouped by relapse status. Kruskal-Wallis test was performed across samples from patients of different relapse status within each copy-number signature group. Statistical significance was observed for signatures 2 (related to poor overall survival, due to tandem duplication through CDK12 inactivation; p = 0.00021), 3 (related to favorable overall survival, mechanism of BRCA1/2-related HRD; p = 0.0011), 4 (related to poor overall survival, due to whole-genome duplication related to failure of cell-cycle control and phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase (PI3K) inactivation; p = 0.00047), and 6 (related to poor overall survival, mechanism proposed of focal amplification due to failure of cell-cycle control; p = 0.00032).
seo_title: Copy number signature Chemotherapy response Box Plot, Cell Reports Medicine 2025

caption: d
description: A composite correlation matrix visualization comparing KEGG Orthologs (KOs) against themselves, specific microbial species, and a clinical variable ('Plaque'). Correlation strength (Spearman r) and regression coefficients (Beta coefficient) are encoded by circle size and a red/blue diverging color scale, with asterisks indicating significance (P < 0.05).
legend: Associations between gut microbiota functional enzymes, plaque-associated bacteria species, and carotid artery plaque status. The Spearman correlation heatmap include 11 microbial functional enzymes under carbonnitrogen ligases category and the 6 plaque-associated bacterial species. Associations between the 11 microbial functional enzymes and carotid artery plaque status were estimated by linear regression models after adjustment for age, race, study site, antibiotics use, income, education, BMI, alcohol, smoking status, HIV status, and ART use
seo_title: KEGG Orthologs Microbiome Enzyme Function Correlation Matrix, Microbiome 2025
